creator: danfe@FreeBSD.org
text:
State Changed
From-To: open->analyzed

While there're certainly a reason in your suggestion, I'd rather not 
touch things this very moment.  You see, this port is not just a pure 
driver in X.org's sense.  It combines kernel module, X.org module 
(driver), and a few shared libraries.  While some GNU/Linux 
distributions separate these bits into two or more packages, I currently 
see no strong reason for doing this, as nVidia's libraries are useless 
without kernel modules, and vice versa.  So I think we're actually 
winning here having it as a single port.  And given its "heterogenous" 
natute I think `x11' suits as better place than `x11-drivers' for it.
